title: Geometry, Scale, Similarity, and Spatial Planning · Spiral Review
summary: Create a scale plan for an accessible room layout with paths, clearances, paint, and flooring. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an accessible scale floor plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify angles and two- and three-dimensional shapes by defining properties rather than appearance.
Choose and justify perimeter or area models for borders, surfaces, and composite regions.
Choose volume or surface-area models for capacity, covering, and packaging tasks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

stimulus.title: Similarity Pythagorean case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math beginner geometry scale. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The Pythagorean theorem applies to every triangle.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold. Artifact under review: an accessible scale floor plan.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Similarity Pythagorean, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Similarity Pythagorean applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold.
